completed_tasks.name: Verify routing works end-to-end
completed_tasks.detail: Confirmed: orchestrator calls arc_agent connected-agent tool for 'show my arc enabled servers'. Run step shows: connected_agent: arc_agent | args: {query: 'show my arc enabled servers'}

remaining_tasks.name: Deploy Arc MCP Server (ca-arc-mcp-prod)
remaining_tasks.detail: Arc agent responds 'no direct access' because arc_servers_list tool is only provided by the Arc MCP Server container. Need to deploy it and set ARC_MCP_SERVER_URL env var on ca-arc-prod.

blockers.description: Arc MCP Server not deployed — arc agent can't list/query Arc resources
blockers.workaround: Arc agent works for triage with specific resource IDs; just can't list all servers

human_actions_pending.action: Deploy Arc MCP Server container to ca-arc-mcp-prod and set ARC_MCP_SERVER_URL on ca-arc-prod
human_actions_pending.context: The Arc agent is routed correctly but responds 'no access' for listing Arc resources — Arc MCP Server provides arc_servers_list, arc_extensions_list, arc_k8s_list, arc_k8s_gitops_status

decisions.decision: Use Foundry connected-agent tools (not HandoffOrchestrator) for routing
decisions.rationale: Foundry's native connected_agent tool type enables direct agent-to-agent routing without the orchestrator needing to call a custom tool. The tool name must match ^[a-zA-Z_]+$ so underscores required.

decisions.decision: Register domain agent tools on each Foundry agent definition
decisions.rationale: When orchestrator routes via connected_agent, the target agent's Foundry definition must have its function tools registered — container runtime tools alone are not visible to Foundry's connected-agent execution

decisions.decision: Keep eol in DOMAIN_AGENT_MAP (as eol_agent) even though eol-agent not in Foundry yet
decisions.rationale: Linter/Phase 12 code added eol entries; keeping them avoids regressions. The eol_agent connected-agent tool would simply not be registered on orchestrator until eol-agent is deployed.

next_action: If arc listing is needed: deploy the Arc MCP Server container (services/arc-mcp-server/) as a Container App and set ARC_MCP_SERVER_URL env var on ca-arc-prod. Otherwise routing is fully functional — test other domain queries (compute, network, security) to validate the connected-agent routing works across all domains.
context_notes: Routing was broken because the Foundry orchestrator agent definition had a stale system prompt (from before arc/patch routing was added) and a wrong azure_tools function instead of connected_agent tools. The fix was entirely on the Foundry API side (no container rebuild needed) plus code changes to use underscore tool names. All 192 tests pass. The arc routing works but arc agent can only triage with specific resource IDs — it can't LIST all arc servers without the Arc MCP Server.
